title: SACRAMENTO – Assemblywoman Pilar Schiavo announced today that at the close of the Legislative session, seven key pieces of her authored legislation are now on the Governor’s desk.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The legislation allows for the provision of free ID and vital records to individuals experiencing homelessness or meeting low income program requirements.",
    "The legislation aims to streamline the statewide affordable and homeless housing funding process.",
    "The legislation removes restrictive density requirements prior to the purchase of properties for affordable housing development.",
    "The legislation mandates a year of supportive postpartum care for new mothers enrolled in Medi-Cal.",
    "The legislation accepts electronic signatures for certain medical releases to expedite access to healthcare.",
    "The legislation requires the State Water Board to consider new environmental data for mining projects not ruled on in over thirty years.",
    "The legislation prohibits the manufacture or sale of artificial turf containing toxic forever chemicals (PFAS) starting in 2026.",
    "The legislation designates human trafficking of a minor for commercial sex acts as a serious felony.",
    "The legislation requires schools to keep at least two doses of naloxone hydrochloride on campus for fentanyl overdose emergencies."
  ]
}
```
